TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Technical Architecture Brief
Engineering architecture brief with appendices imported from working papers and scan-heavy captures.
Document ID
TEC-001
Owner
Platform Engineering
Version
0.8 draft
Prepared Date
2024-02-29
Status
Engineering review draft
Classification
Internal / Technical
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
1. Objective and Scope
This brief describes the architecture of Meridian’s Knowledge Operations Platform, including the vendor-managed retrieval
layer, internal policy services, content pipelines, and observability controls used for the Q2 2024 pilot.
●
Scope includes retrieval path, orchestration, document ingestion, policy retrieval, and logging boundaries.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-01
1. Objective and Scope — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-01
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 2]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 2
2. System Context
The platform combines internal indexing pipelines with the Northwind managed retrieval cluster. User traffic enters through
Meridian identity and gateway services before requests are brokered to retrieval and summarisation components.
●
Primary dependencies: SSO, API gateway, Northwind cluster, internal policy service, event telemetry.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-02
2. System Context — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-02
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 3]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 3
3. Data Classification and Routing
Corporate data is classified at ingestion. Regulated or confidential content is routed to approved stores, and non-production
experimentation uses synthetic or anonymised material only.
●
Routing labels drive storage tier, retention policy, and retrieval permissions.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-03
3. Data Classification and Routing — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-03
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 4]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 4
4. Retrieval Pipeline
Documents are chunked, embedded, indexed, and routed through reranking and answer-generation layers. The design
intentionally separates retrieval from generation to preserve evidence and auditability.
●
Key controls: chunk metadata, repository lineage, grounded answer citations.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-04
4. Retrieval Pipeline — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-04
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 5]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 5
5. Security Controls
Authentication, authorisation, logging, and change review are enforced at the Meridian boundary. Vendor-managed
components supply telemetry and admin-activity exports to Meridian security tooling.
●
Quarterly privileged review and admin approval evidence are required for vendor support access.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-05
5. Security Controls — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-05
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 6]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 6
6. Availability and Recovery
The initial pilot targets operational resilience through fallback search paths and dual-run validation, while commercial SLA
negotiations remain open.
●
Current vendor SLA is 99.5% monthly with a temporary pilot-phase exception request in place.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-06
6. Availability and Recovery — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-06
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 7]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 7
7. Model and Evaluation Layer
Generation and retrieval settings are evaluated against internal groundedness, adherence, and latency criteria. Structured
extraction tasks share the same evidence-bearing retrieval layer.
●
Primary tasks include extraction, summary, checklist, contract risk extraction, and CV analysis.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-07
7. Model and Evaluation Layer — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-07
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 8]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 8
8. Open Risks
Open issues include vendor liability, transfer mechanisms for EU analytics data, and explicit incident-notification timing.
These issues are tracked outside the architecture design but affect go-live gating.
●
The technical design itself remains viable; go-live is conditional on non-technical remediation.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-08
8. Open Risks — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-08
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 9]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 9
9. Operational Readiness
Cutover requires validated ingestion, observability, dashboard alerts, on-call runbooks, and approved evidence routing. The
pilot also depends on archiving technical annexes and diagrams into the document library.
●
Pages 12-15 intentionally preserve scan-heavy working annexes to test OCR-routing behavior.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-09
9. Operational Readiness — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-09
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 10]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 10
10. Appendix Index
Appendices include network capture diagrams, control mapping notes, and engineering whiteboard snapshots. Several
pages are image-only imports from working papers and may require OCR fallback.
●
The frontend demo intentionally models these pages as difficult for OCR and indexing.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-10
10. Appendix Index — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-10
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

[Page 11]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
TEC-001  Technical Architecture Brief
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 11
11. Change Log
Draft 0.8 integrates vendor-review feedback, adds policy-service boundaries, and records the need to preserve
evidence-bearing document lineage in downstream workflows.
●
Next revision expected after the procurement and privacy gates are cleared.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-11
11. Change Log — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.
TEC-CTRL-11
Control dependency reviewed alongside policy, contract, and evidence workflows.

This brief describes the architecture of Meridian’s Knowledge Operations Platform, including the vendor-managed retrieval

2. System Context
The platform combines internal indexing pipelines with the Northwind managed retrieval cluster. User traffic enters through
Meridian identity and gateway services before requests are brokered to retrieval and summarisation components.
●
Primary dependencies: SSO, API gateway, Northwind cluster, internal policy service, event telemetry.
Technical Reference
Detail

2. System Context — architecture note maintained by Platform Engineering.

Corporate data is classified at ingestion. Regulated or confidential content is routed to approved stores, and non-production
experimentation uses synthetic or anonymised material only.
●
Routing labels drive storage tier, retention policy, and retrieval permissions.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-03

Documents are chunked, embedded, indexed, and routed through reranking and answer-generation layers. The design
intentionally separates retrieval from generation to preserve evidence and auditability.
●
Key controls: chunk metadata, repository lineage, grounded answer citations.
Technical Reference
Detail

1. Objective and Scope
This brief describes the architecture of Meridian’s Knowledge Operations Platform, including the vendor-managed retrieval
layer, internal policy services, content pipelines, and observability controls used for the Q2 2024 pilot.
●
Scope includes retrieval path, orchestration, document ingestion, policy retrieval, and logging boundaries.

2. System Context
The platform combines internal indexing pipelines with the Northwind managed retrieval cluster. User traffic enters through
Meridian identity and gateway services before requests are brokered to retrieval and summarisation components.
●
Primary dependencies: SSO, API gateway, Northwind cluster, internal policy service, event telemetry.

Detail

3. Data Classification and Routing
Corporate data is classified at ingestion. Regulated or confidential content is routed to approved stores, and non-production
experimentation uses synthetic or anonymised material only.
●
Routing labels drive storage tier, retention policy, and retrieval permissions.
Technical Reference
Detail
TEC-REF-03

4. Retrieval Pipeline
Documents are chunked, embedded, indexed, and routed through reranking and answer-generation layers. The design
intentionally separates retrieval from generation to preserve evidence and auditability.
●
Key controls: chunk metadata, repository lineage, grounded answer citations.
